description: Assessment-as-Quest Embedding — Embed assessment data in quest customData and store results in save file instead of AssessmentSession collection

userStories.title: Define AssessmentQuestData type for quest customData
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a type for embedding assessment definitions in quest customData so the UI can read them without hardcoded encounter files.
acceptanceCriteria: Create AssessmentQuestData type in shared/assessment/assessment-types.ts that contains: phases[] (each with id, type, name, tasks[], maxScore, scoringDimensions[]), totalMaxPoints, assessmentType, and estimatedMinutes
The type matches the shape of existing AssessmentDefinition so the UI can consume it without changes
Each task within a phase includes: id, type, prompt, maxPoints, scoringMethod, scoringDimensions[], contentTemplate, and optional populated fields: passage, questions[], writingPrompts[]
Create AssessmentPhaseResult type for storing player answers: phaseId, score, maxScore, taskResults[] (each with taskId, playerAnswer, score, maxPoints), dimensionScores, completedAt
Create AssessmentCompletionResult type: totalScore, maxScore, cefrLevel, dimensionScores, completedAt
Export all types from assessment-types.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These types are consumed by all subsequent stories. Keep them in the existing assessment-types.ts file alongside the current types.

userStories.title: Generate arrival assessment content at world creation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need arrival assessment content (passages, questions, writing prompts) generated and baked into the quest template at world creation time.
acceptanceCriteria: In quest-chain-templates.ts (or quest-seed-generator.ts), when creating the Arrival Assessment quest, call the LLM content generator for each phase's tasks
Generated content stored in quest customData.assessment as AssessmentQuestData
Reading phase: passage and questions[] with answer options populated in task data
Writing phase: writingPrompts[] populated in task data
Listening phase: passage and questions[] populated (audio generated on-demand later via TTS)
Content templates ({{targetLanguage}}, {{cityName}}) resolved using world data before LLM generation
If LLM generation fails for any phase, fall back to the hardcoded ARRIVAL_ENCOUNTER content from arrival-encounter.ts
Conversation and initiate_conversation phases use existing questConfig (no LLM content needed)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use the existing assessment-scoring.ts LLM content generation functions. Batch the calls for reading, writing, and listening phases. The encounter file is the fallback, not the primary source.

userStories.title: Generate departure and periodic assessment content at world creation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need departure and periodic assessment content pre-generated and embedded in their quest templates at world creation time.
acceptanceCriteria: Departure assessment quest created with customData.assessment containing all 4 phases (reading, writing, listening, conversation) with pre-generated content
Periodic assessment quests created with customData.assessment containing the conversational phase with pre-generated content
Departure content uses intermediate difficulty (matching DEPARTURE_ENCOUNTER)
Periodic content is CEFR-adaptive based on the milestone level (5, 10, 15, 20)
If LLM generation fails, fall back to hardcoded encounter file content (departure-encounter.ts, periodic-encounter.ts)
All assessment quest types stored in the world's quest collection with customData.assessment populated
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Departure quest starts as 'unavailable' and is unlocked after 10 quests completed. Periodic quests start as 'unavailable' and unlock at level milestones. Content is pre-generated regardless of when the player reaches them.

userStories.title: Read assessment data from quest customData in game client
userStories.description: As a player, when I start an assessment, the game should read phase/task definitions from the quest's customData instead of the hardcoded encounter files.
acceptanceCriteria: The assessment UI code reads quest.customData.assessment for phase definitions and pre-generated content
If customData.assessment is missing (legacy quests), fall back to the encounter definition files (arrival-encounter.ts, etc.)
The AssessmentModalConfig passed to the reading/writing/listening modals contains the same fields as before: passage, questions, writingPrompts
Reading phase modal displays the quest-embedded passage and questions correctly
Writing phase modal displays the quest-embedded writing prompts correctly
Listening phase modal displays the quest-embedded passage and questions correctly
Conversation phase still triggers NPC interaction as before (no change needed)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The UI components themselves don't change — only where they get their data from changes. The fallback to encounter files ensures backward compatibility with quests created before this change.

userStories.title: Store player answers and phase scores in quest overlay
userStories.description: As a player, when I complete assessment phases, my answers and scores should be saved in the quest overlay (save file) instead of the AssessmentSession collection.
acceptanceCriteria: When a phase is completed, call questOverlay.updateQuest(questId, { phaseResults: [...] }) with the AssessmentPhaseResult data
Phase results include: phaseId, score, maxScore, taskResults[] (each with taskId, playerAnswer, score, maxPoints), dimensionScores, completedAt
The quest overlay overrides map stores the full phaseResults array alongside the quest status
No POST /api/assessments/:sessionId/phases/:phaseId calls are made during gameplay
Results persist correctly across save/load cycles (verify by saving, reloading, and checking phase results are restored)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The quest overlay already supports arbitrary fields in overrides. phaseResults is just another field stored alongside status. The existing questOverlay.serialize()/deserialize() handles this automatically.

userStories.title: Complete assessment via quest overlay with CEFR scoring
userStories.description: As a player, when I finish all assessment phases, the quest should be marked complete with final CEFR scoring stored in the save file.
acceptanceCriteria: When all phase objectives are marked complete, call questOverlay.updateQuest(questId, { status: 'completed', assessmentResult: {...} })
assessmentResult uses AssessmentCompletionResult type: totalScore, maxScore, cefrLevel, dimensionScores, completedAt
CEFR level is computed client-side using shared/assessment/cefr-mapping.ts (existing mapScoreToCEFR logic)
No PUT /api/assessments/:sessionId/complete calls are made during gameplay
The player's CEFR level is updated in the save state (currentState.player.cefrLevel)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This replaces the server-side assessment completion flow. The CEFR computation logic already exists in shared/ and can be called client-side.

userStories.title: Update report card to read from quest overlays
userStories.description: As a player, the learning report card should compare arrival and departure results from quest overlays instead of the AssessmentSession collection.
acceptanceCriteria: Report card generation (LearningReportCard) reads arrival assessment results from the arrival quest's overlay (phaseResults + assessmentResult)
Report card reads departure assessment results from the departure quest's overlay
Phase deltas (per-phase score improvement) computed by comparing matching phases between arrival and departure
Dimension deltas (per-dimension improvement) computed from dimensionScores in both results
cefrImproved boolean correctly reflects whether departure CEFR > arrival CEFR
Report card data structure matches existing LearningReportCard type
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The report card is generated when the departure assessment completes. It needs access to both quest overlays — find the arrival quest by questType='assessment' + tags including 'arrival', and the departure quest by tags including 'departure'.

userStories.title: Per-phase quest objectives with concrete completion triggers
userStories.description: As a player, I need the Arrival Assessment quest to have separate objectives for each assessment phase so I can see progress and the system knows when I'm done.
acceptanceCriteria: The Arrival Assessment quest is created with 4 objectives: obj_reading (Complete reading comprehension), obj_writing (Complete writing assessment), obj_listening (Complete listening comprehension), obj_speaking (Complete conversation assessment)
Each objective has a completionTrigger field specifying the event that marks it complete (reading_phase_submitted, writing_phase_submitted, listening_phase_submitted, conversation_phase_completed)
When the assessment UI submits a phase, the corresponding objective is marked completed: true in the quest overlay
The quest is marked status: 'completed' only when ALL 4 objectives are complete
Quest progress percentage is calculated as (completedObjectives / totalObjectives) * 100
The Prolog content uses per-objective completion checks, not a generic Progress >= 100
Departure and periodic assessments similarly have per-phase objectives
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This replaces the single vague objective with concrete per-phase objectives. The quest seed in quest-chain-templates.ts must generate these objectives. The assessment UI must fire the right completion trigger when each phase is submitted.

userStories.title: Remove quest chain dependency from assessments
userStories.description: As a developer, assessment quests should be self-contained with no quest_chain references since all data is embedded in customData.
acceptanceCriteria: The Arrival Assessment quest is created without a quest_chain or chainTemplateId reference
Assessment quest Prolog content does not include quest_chain/2 or quest_chain_order/2 predicates
The completionCriteria field uses { type: 'all_objectives' } instead of { assessmentDefinitionId: 'arrival_encounter' }
Assessment quests still have appropriate tags (assessment, arrival, main-quest, narrative) for identification
Departure and periodic assessment quests are similarly self-contained
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Clean up quest-chain-templates.ts so assessment quests don't reference chain IDs. The chain was an artifact of the old system where assessments were part of a linear quest chain.

userStories.title: Score analysis and CEFR calibration from assessment results
userStories.description: As a player, after completing the arrival assessment, my scores should be analyzed to determine my CEFR level and calibrate the game's language difficulty.
acceptanceCriteria: After all phase objectives are complete, the system computes per-dimension scores (comprehension, vocabulary, grammar, fluency) from stored phaseResults
Total score is mapped to a CEFR level using the existing mapScoreToCEFR() function from shared/assessment/cefr-mapping.ts
The computed CEFR level is stored in the quest overlay assessmentResult.cefrLevel AND in currentState.player.cefrLevel (save state)

The game language difficulty systems read from currentState.player.cefrLevel on load, adjusting: NPC prompt language mode (simplified/bilingual/natural), vocabulary frequency constraints, and quest difficulty gating
If player scores A1, NPCs use SIMPLIFIED mode; A2-B1 use BILINGUAL; B2+ use NATURAL
The assessment result is visible in the player's journal/quest log
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the critical feedback loop — assessment results must actually affect gameplay. The CEFR level drives NPC language mode in context-manager.ts via the cefrLevel field in the conversation context.

userStories.title: Remove gameplay writes to AssessmentSession collection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to ensure the game client never writes to the AssessmentSession MongoDB collection during gameplay.
acceptanceCriteria: No POST /api/assessments calls from the game client during gameplay
No PUT /api/assessments/:sessionId/phases/:phaseId calls from the game client
No PUT /api/assessments/:sessionId/complete calls from the game client
No PUT /api/assessments/:sessionId/recordings calls from the game client
All assessment data flows through the quest overlay to save file pipeline
The AssessmentSession collection remains for historical data but receives no new writes from gameplay
Search all game client files for /api/assessments write calls and remove or redirect them
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the verification/cleanup story. Grep for all /api/assessments calls in shared/game-engine/ and client/src/components/3DGame/ and ensure none are write operations during gameplay.

userStories.title: Update admin assessment endpoints to read from playthrough data
userStories.description: As an admin, I want assessment analytics endpoints to read from playthrough quest data instead of the legacy AssessmentSession collection.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/assessments/player/:playerId reads assessment results from playthrough quest overlays (finds assessment quests and reads their overlay data)
GET /api/assessments/world/:worldId/summary aggregates assessment results across all playthroughs for the world
GET /api/assessments/:sessionId/transcripts reads transcript data from quest overlay phaseResults
Write endpoints (POST /api/assessments, PUT /api/assessments/:sessionId/phases/:phaseId, PUT /api/assessments/:sessionId/complete) return 410 Gone with a message explaining the migration
Read endpoints return data in the same JSON format as before for backward compatibility with any admin UI
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The admin endpoints need to query playthroughs, find assessment-type quests, and read their overlay data. This is a server-side change in server/routes/assessment-routes.ts.

userStories.title: Persist reading progress in game save
userStories.description: As a player, I want my reading progress (which articles I've read, quiz answers, comprehension scores) to persist across save/load cycles.
acceptanceCriteria: Add readingProgress field to GameSaveState: Record<textId, { read: boolean, answeredQuestionIds: string[], comprehensionScore: number, readAt: string }>
Add getReadingProgress() to createGameStateSource() in BabylonGame — collects reading state from the notices/library system and returns it
Add restoreReadingProgress() to createGameStateTarget() in BabylonGame — restores read status and answered questions on load
The Library/Notices tab correctly shows articles as read/unread after a save/load cycle
Comprehension quiz answers persist — already-answered questions show their results instead of being re-answerable
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The game already tracks answered articles via a Set (answeredNoticeQuestions). This needs to be serialized into the save state. Check BabylonGame's notice/article tracking and GameMenuSystem's getAnsweredArticleIds().

userStories.title: Persist NPC contact list and met status in game save
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see which NPCs I've met and my relationship with them preserved across save/load cycles.
acceptanceCriteria: Add contacts field to GameSaveState: Record<npcId, { met: boolean, firstMetAt: string, conversationCount: number, lastSpokenAt: string, disposition: number }>
Add getContacts() to createGameStateSource() in BabylonGame — collects NPC met status from the greeting system, conversation history, and relationship manager
Add restoreContacts() to createGameStateTarget() in BabylonGame — restores NPC met flags and conversation counts on load
The Contacts tab correctly shows previously-met NPCs after a save/load cycle
NPCs the player has never spoken to do not appear in the contact list
Conversation count per NPC persists correctly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently contacts are derived from conversation history and relationships at runtime. The fix needs to explicitly track met status. The NPCGreetingSystem already has hasMet/markAsMet — wire that into the save state.

userStories.title: Persist conversation history in game save
userStories.description: As a player, I want my past NPC conversation records to persist so the game can reference them for context and the contact list shows accurate data.
acceptanceCriteria: Add conversations field to GameSaveState: Array<{ npcId: string, npcName: string, timestamp: string, turnCount: number, wordsUsed: string[], targetLanguagePercent: number, fluencyGained: number }>
Add getConversationHistory() to createGameStateSource() in BabylonGame — collects ConversationRecord array from the LanguageProgressTracker
Add restoreConversationHistory() to createGameStateTarget() in BabylonGame — restores conversation records into the tracker on load
Limit saved conversations to the most recent 50 per playthrough to keep save file size manageable
Conversation count in the Contacts tab reflects actual conversations had (not reset on reload)
The language progress tracker's conversationRecords array is populated on load from saved data
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The LanguageProgressTracker already has a conversationRecords array. It needs a restoreConversationRecords() method and the save state source needs to serialize it. Currently conversations are synced to the server separately — this adds them to the local save snapshot too.

userStories.title: Persist known NPC details in game save
userStories.description: As a player, I want facts I've learned about NPCs during conversations (occupation, family, interests) to persist across save/load cycles.
acceptanceCriteria: Add npcKnownDetails field to GameSaveState: Record<npcId, { facts: string[], learnedAt: string[] }>
Add getNpcKnownDetails() to createGameStateSource() in BabylonGame — collects known details from the conversation metadata extraction system
Add restoreNpcKnownDetails() to createGameStateTarget() in BabylonGame — restores known details on load
The Contacts tab shows previously-learned facts about NPCs after a save/load cycle
New facts learned during conversation are appended (not duplicated) to existing known details
Limit to 20 facts per NPC to keep save file size manageable
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Known details come from the conversation metadata extraction (background LLM call after each chat exchange). The extraction produces structured info about the NPC. This needs to accumulate in a persistent store rather than being ephemeral.

userStories.title: Resolve quest objective placeholders to actual entity IDs
userStories.description: As a player, quest objectives that reference {npc}, {location}, etc. should resolve to actual NPC names and location names so I know who to talk to and where to go.
acceptanceCriteria: During quest seed generation, all {npc}, {npc_0}, {npc_1}, {location}, {location_0}, {destination} placeholders in objective descriptions are resolved to actual character names and location names from the world data
Objective npcId fields are populated with actual character IDs (not placeholder strings) so talk_to_npc matching works
Objective locationName fields are populated with actual location/settlement names so visit_location matching works
If a required NPC or location doesn't exist in the world, the quest is not generated (skip rather than create broken quest)
Quest descriptions also have placeholders resolved (e.g., 'Talk to {npc}' becomes 'Talk to Marie Robichaux')
The {targetLanguage} placeholder is resolved to the world's target language name
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The quest seed generator creates quests with placeholder strings. These need to be resolved during seedQuests() or distributeRadiantQuests() using the world's character and settlement data. Check quest-seed-generator.ts and quest-assignment-engine.ts for where placeholders are inserted.

userStories.title: Wire restaurant food ordering into quest completion
userStories.description: As a player, when I order food at a restaurant NPC, the food_ordered event should fire so order_food quest objectives complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify that restaurant/food businesses exist in the generated world (Tavern, Café, etc.)
When the player interacts with a food merchant NPC and places an order, the food_ordered event fires with itemName, merchantId, and businessType
The order_food objective type in quests like 'Lunch Order' and 'Dinner Party' progresses when food_ordered fires
If no food ordering system exists, add a basic one: player opens shop at food business, selects food item, event fires
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check if NPCBusinessInteractionSystem has a food ordering flow. The event type food_ordered is defined in QuestCompletionEngine but may not be emitted by any game system. Also check if food items exist in merchant inventories.

userStories.title: Wire merchant haggling into quest completion
userStories.description: As a player, when I negotiate prices with a merchant in the target language, the price_haggled event should fire so haggle_price quest objectives complete.
acceptanceCriteria: When the player uses bargaining/price-related words during merchant conversation, the price_haggled event fires
The haggle_price objective type in quests like 'Bargain Hunter' and 'Haggling' progresses when price_haggled fires
The conversation system detects haggling intent (price words, negotiation phrases) and emits the event
If no haggling detection exists, add keyword matching for price/number words in merchant conversations
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The price_haggled event is expected by the quest system but may not be emitted during normal merchant interactions. Check if the conversation metadata extraction or topic tagging detects haggling intent.

userStories.title: Wire navigation waypoint system into quest completion
userStories.description: As a player, when I follow directions to reach waypoints, the direction_step_completed event should fire so follow_directions and navigate_language quest objectives complete.
acceptanceCriteria: When the player reaches a waypoint marker during a navigation quest, the direction_step_completed event fires
The follow_directions and navigate_language objective types progress when direction_step_completed fires
Navigation quests place visible waypoint markers in the world that the player can walk to
Reaching a waypoint triggers the next direction step (or completes the objective if it was the last one)
If no waypoint system exists, add basic proximity-trigger waypoints that fire the event when the player walks within 3m
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check QuestWaypointManager and QuestHotspotManager for existing waypoint infrastructure. The direction_step_completed event may already fire from these systems.

userStories.title: Make chapter meta-objectives trackable with clear progress indicators
userStories.description: As a player, I need to understand what counts toward chapter meta-objectives (vocabulary, conversation, grammar) and see my progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Chapter quest objectives with type 'vocabulary', 'conversation', or 'grammar' show a progress counter in the quest tracker HUD (e.g., '2/5 vocabulary activities')
The quest tracker tooltip or description explains what counts: vocabulary = any word learned or used, conversation = any NPC chat opened, grammar = any positive grammar feedback
Progress increments visibly when the player performs the relevant action (toast or HUD update)
Each meta-objective has a reasonable required count based on the chapter's difficulty level
The collect_text meta-objective shows how many texts have been found vs required
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The meta-objective mappings (vocabulary→vocabulary_usage, conversation→npc_talked, grammar→grammar_demonstrated) already work technically. This story is about making them visible and understandable to the player through the quest tracker UI.
